metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full (was bug; #659 parser fix maps the type:"info" 'Request cancelled.' cancel notice to turn_done so the ESC-cancelled turn settles ready instead of sticking in working); driver_capability: ready. 2026-06-13 recording confirms the correct ready→working→ready arc; verify 6/6.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

Route: record (the #659 parser fix landed; the cell records the correct ready → working → ready arc and is no longer known_failing).

## Scenario

user-esc-interrupt: the user cancels a long in-flight turn with ESC. User-observable signal (acceptance_criteria + process): ready → working → ready; the cancelled turn settles back to ready and does NOT stick in working post-interrupt; a follow-up prompt then drives a clean ready → working → ready recovery arc on the same session, never entering waiting.

## agent_supports: yes

Gemini CLI binds ESC to cancel the in-flight turn. The cancel path runs through the interactive UI's handleUserCancelledEvent: on the abort signal the stream yields GeminiEventType.UserCancelled (processGeminiStreamEvents returns status 1), the handler flushes any pending history item, then appends addItem({ type: "info", text: "Request cancelled." }), calls setIsResponding(false), and returns control to the prompt. Only the in-flight turn aborts; the process stays alive. So ESC cancellation is a real, shipped feature (v0.46.0), confirmed live in the 2026-06-13 recording.

## daemon_capability: full (was: bug, fixed by #659)

Previously daemon=bug: a durable trace of the cancel existed on disk (one type:"info" 'Request cancelled.' MessageRecord) but the gemini-cli parser dropped it. parseMessage dispatched strictly on type — user→parseUser, gemini→parseAssistant, default (incl. info/error/warning) → return false → ev.Skip — and turn_done was inferred ONLY from a final text-only gemini message with no open tool calls. A cancelled turn never produces that message (processGeminiStreamEvents returns UserCancelled → the caller early-returns before the post-stream recordMessage({type:"gemini"}) flush), and the partial gemini item flushed during cancel is not recorded (the chat-recording switch breaks for gemini). So no turn_done fired, IsAgentDone() never tripped, and with no inactivity sweep the session stuck in working until the recovery turn's terminal gemini message landed.

Fix (#659): parser.go now routes type:"info" through parseInfo, which maps the cancel notice (content == "Request cancelled.") to EventType turn_done; every other info notice (compression, system chatter) is still dropped so a mid-turn notice cannot prematurely settle a still-working session. With the cancel notice now emitting turn_done, IsAgentDone() fires and the classifier settles the open turn working → ready. This is the same shape as the codex turn-aborted handling (which keys off a structural turn_aborted marker). Signal present + daemon now handles it → full.

## Live recording evidence (2026-06-13, irrlichd 0.5.1+68aba13, this branch)

The recording proves the fix. State arc on the SAME session (session-2026-06-13T12-33...):

- ready (new session) → working (long count-to-100 prompt lands)
- the ESC was sent ~8s into the turn; gemini flushed the 'Request cancelled.' (type:"info") notice to the chat file ~8s later; the parser mapped it to turn_done and the session settled working → ready ~3s after the notice appeared — it did NOT stick in working
- recovery prompt ('Reply with exactly the word: ok') drives ready → working → ready (terminal gemini message 'ok')
- never entered waiting

Verify: 6/6 phases pass (session_birth, pid_bind, turn_start, interrupt_to_ready, recovery_start, recovery_done). model=gemini-3.5-flash, non-zero cost + tokens.

## driver_capability: ready

The recipe's ESC interrupt is delivered via the keys step (tmux send-keys Escape), which the gemini-cli interactive driver implements (keys is in DRIVE_ELICITS); send/slash/wait_turn/sleep are all real. The wait_turn step times out counting only 1 terminal gemini message (the cancelled turn emits an info notice, not a gemini message), but that is a driver turn-counter artifact — the events.jsonl + transcript capture the full correct arc, and verify passes 6/6.
caveats: daemon=full is confirmed by the 2026-06-13 live recording (irrlichd 0.5.1+68aba13, this branch): the type:"info" 'Request cancelled.' notice is mapped to turn_done by the #659 parseInfo fix, settling the cancelled turn working → ready on the same session before the recovery turn. Confidence raised from 0.75 to 0.95.

Two sub-cases share the cancel path: ESC during pure text streaming (this cell — only the type:"info" notice lands) and ESC during an open tool call (the tool is also rewritten to status:"cancelled"). This cell exercises the pure-text-stream flavour; both now settle to ready under the #659 fix (the tool sub-case additionally closes the open tool via parser.go).

The interrupt_to_ready spec phase carries no max_delay_ms: the cancel-notice flush latency is a property of what gemini writes (the recipe sleeps 8s before the ESC and the notice flushed ~8s later in the recording), so the meaningful assertion is the same-session settle ordering (settles to ready before the recovery turn), matching the sibling turn-aborted-by-error settle phase.

The driver's wait_turn times out on this scenario because it counts terminal gemini messages and the cancelled turn emits an info notice rather than a gemini message (count=1, expected ≥ 2). This is a turn-counter artifact, not a capture failure — the events.jsonl + transcript hold the full correct arc and verify passes 6/6.

sources.ref: 68aba139 fix(geminicli): settle turn on ESC cancel notice (#659)
sources.note: Adds parser.go parseInfo: maps a type:"info" notice whose content == "Request cancelled." to EventType turn_done; every other info notice is dropped. This settles an ESC-cancelled turn working → ready instead of sticking in working.

parseMessage now dispatches info → parseInfo (line 106-107); parseInfo (line 120-126) sets ev.EventType="turn_done" for content=="Request cancelled." The cancel notice is no longer skipped, so turn_done fires and the classifier settles the turn to ready.

Live evidence (this branch): the SAME session goes ready → working → ready at the ESC cancel (settle ~3s after the 'Request cancelled.' notice flushed) then ready → working → ready for the recovery turn; never waiting. Verify 6/6.
Sibling cell with the identical root cause (the parser skipping a non-user/gemini MessageRecord). Its settle phase also carries no max_delay_ms; this cell follows the same pattern.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH and authenticated (oauth-personal or API key)
gemini version where ESC cancels the in-flight turn (confirmed v0.46.0)
the ESC interrupt is delivered via the keys step (tmux send-keys Escape) — the keys primitive is ported in the gemini-cli driver
irrlichd is recording (run-cell.sh isolated, or --attach against a daemon started with --record)
setup: Launch gemini interactively under tmux (gemini -y) so the process outlives the daemon observation window — NOT headless gemini -p, which exits at turn end
Empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ so the session is fresh

script.text: Count slowly from 1 to 100, with a brief one-sentence explanation for each number. Take your time and stream the response.
Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered for the launcher bound by cwd shortly after launch
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new / session in ready shortly after launch
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to working after the long prompt lands
transcript session-*.jsonl contains a user-cancelled trace (type:"info" 'User cancelled the request.' MessageRecord) for the cancelled turn

KNOWN_FAILING: events.jsonl is EXPECTED to NOT contain a working→ready settle for the cancelled turn (parser skips type:"info" → no turn_done) — the session sticks in working until the recovery prompt
the recovery prompt drives a clean ready→working→ready arc (proving the parser reset once a terminal gemini message lands)
